items.title: Antonelli's participation in qualifying in doubt after huge crash
items.summary: Kimi Antonelli's participation in Australian Grand Prix qualifying is in doubt after the Italian severely damaged his Mercedes in a huge crash in final practice at the first round of the 2026 Formula 1 season.

items.title: Slot hails 'special' Ngumoha as Liverpool reach QF
items.summary: Liverpool manager Arne Slot has heaped praise on "special" Rio Ngumoha after the teenager impressed in his side's 3-1 victory over Wolves on Friday.

items.title: Valverde puts Madrid 'on his back' for vital win
items.summary: Álvaro Arbeloa called Federico Valverde "everything a Real Madrid player should be" after the midfielder scored a late winner against Celta Vigo.

items.title: USL season kicks off with on-field CBA protest
items.summary: The USL Championship season opened Friday, but play halted immediately as Lexington SC and Louisville City FC players stood motionless for one minute to protest stalled CBA negotiations.

items.title: Ronaldo injury 'more serious' than expected
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo will travel to Madrid to undergo treatment on his right hamstring injury, Al Nassr coach Jorge Jesus confirmed on Friday.

items.title: Can the old Liverpool resurface again? Salah and Robertson suggest so in FA Cup
items.summary: Liverpool's aggressive win over Wolves in the FA Cup shows that Liverpool can still turn back the clock, despite their recent struggles.

items.title: Australian GP: Russell sets stunning pace after Antonelli crash LIVE!
'Slot needs something to keep him in a job' - could the FA Cup be it?
items.summary: Despite a torrid Premier League campaign, could winning the FA Cup keep Liverpool manager Arne Slot in a job?

items.title: European football: Monaco dent PSG title hopes with victory at Parc des Princes
items.summary: Injury-hit Real Madrid snatch victory at Celta Vigo Bayern Munich extend their lead at top of Bundesliga Monaco opened up the Ligue 1 title race by inflicting a first home defeat of the season on the defending champions, Paris Saint-Germain on Friday night. Maghnes Akliouche had Monaco ahead at half-time, with Aleksandr Golovin and Folarin Balogun scoring after the break for the visitors, while Bradley Barcola grabbed a consolation for PSG, who are still four points clear of second-placed Lens in the standings. Lens, however, have a chance to cut into that lead when they host bottom-placed Metz on Sunday. Continue reading...

items.title: Joao Pedro same level as Haaland and Kane - Rosenior
items.summary: Chelsea head coach Liam Rosenior says striker Joao Pedro belongs in the same "category" as world‑class forwards Erling Haaland, Harry Kane and Kylian Mbappe.

items.title: Spurs could turn to De Zerbi - Saturday's gossip
items.summary: Several names - including those of two former club heroes - are in the running to take the reins at Tottenham, while Serie A champions Napoli are looking to secure the future of Scott McTominay.

items.title: Opmerkelijk: opgestapte Demirel keert na 90 dagen terug als trainer van Turkse club
items.summary: Gençlerbirligi heeft Volkan Demirel aangesteld als de nieuwe hoofdtrainer. De club uit Ankara beargumenteerde deze keuze door te stellen dat Demirel 'onze clubstructuur begrijpt'. Dat kan kloppen: de oud-keeper vertrok amper drie maanden geleden nog als hoofdcoach van de club.

items.title: Real Madrid wendt in absolute slotfase derde puntenverlies op rij af
items.summary: Real Madrid heeft vrijdagavond op het nippertje het derde puntenverlies op rij afgewend. Op bezoek bij Celta de Vigo had De Koninklijke het enorm lastig, maar in de allerlaatste tellen van de wedstrijd ontsnapten de Madrilenen door aanvoerder Federico Valverde.

items.title: Slot looft routinier Robertson en jongeling Ngumoha na revanche op Wolves
items.summary: Liverpool slaagde er vrijdagavond in de FA Cup wel in om af te rekenen met Wolverhampton Wanderers . Drie dagen eerder was de hekkensluiter van de Premier League de regerend landskampioen nog de baas. Het gemoed van trainer Arne Slot was dan ook een stuk beter na afloop.

items.title: Mikel Arteta keeps focus to ensure Stag party doesn’t end in tears for Arsenal
items.summary: Manager knows his quadruple-chasers have a target on their back in their FA Cup fifth round trip to Mansfield Mikel Arteta knows the score. There is a reason why Arsenal’s trip to Mansfield Town on Saturday is the tie of the FA Cup fifth round, why it has been selected by TNT Sports for a 12.15pm kick-off. It has all the ingredients and everybody – Arsenal fans aside – is looking for an upset. Arteta was asked whether he was clear on that point. “Yes,” the Arsenal manager replied. It has always been this way when a top club visits a minnow and, to repeat, the plotlines are certainly there for this one. Mansfield are 16th in League One, too close to the relegation line for the comfort of the manager, Nigel Clough. The Stags have gone nine league matches without a win. Continue reading...

items.title: He was meant to take De Bruyne’s crown. Instead, Foden’s City career is flatlining
items.summary: Playmaker has become peripheral as Manchester City chase quadruple and is no longer a shoo-in for the World Cup With 76 minutes gone at the Etihad Stadium on Wednesday night, Phil Foden was culpable for what might prove the title race’s defining moment. With Manchester City leading Nottingham Forest 2-1, Foden lost Elliot Anderson, who ran off him and curled home a 20-yard equaliser. Sixty seconds later, Pep Guardiola substituted his England playmaker. As Morgan Gibbs-White’s first equaliser could also be traced back to a loose Foden touch, this was a miserable evening for him: City managed only a draw, and as Arsenal won at Brighton, the title race tilted the Gunners’ way. Continue reading...

items.title: Falkirk beat Dundee United to reach Scottish Cup semi-finals
items.summary: Barney Stewart continued his impressive goalscoring form to send Falkirk on their way to the Scottish Cup semi-finals with the opener in a 2-1 victory over Dundee United.

items.title: Robertson and Salah help Liverpool to redemption over Wolves in FA Cup tie
items.summary: This time around, Liverpool made no mistake at Molineux. They stayed on the road to Wembley, thanks to two players signed in 2017, beloved veterans with uncertain futures. Andy Robertson and Mohamed Salah scored the goals to ease the pain of Tuesday’s Premier League defeat before Curtis Jones’s strike completed a night of relative comfort. Wolves crashed back to the reality of eight remaining top-flight matches before the drop. Their goal from Hwang Hee-chan came far too late. While Arne Slot expresses disappointment at the Premier League’s entertainment levels, a verdict hostage to fortune on Tuesday, his starting selection suggested an FA Cup exit would not be countenanced. Winning the trophy will not offer full job security but losing twice at the top flight’s bottom club in three days, a potentially telling blow, was avoided. Continue reading...

items.title: Boëtius is terug: 'Als ik mijn niveau haal, ben ik meerwaarde voor Suriname'
items.summary: Jean-Paul Boëtius maakte vrijdagavond zijn debuut voor RKC Waalwijk. De voormalige Feyenoorder (31) speelde zijn eerste competitiewedstrijd sinds oktober en hoopt daarmee zijn kansen op speelminuten bij de nationale ploeg van Suriname te vergroten.

items.title: Oosting lijkt Champions Play-Offs met Antwerp te kunnen vergeten na gelijkspel
items.summary: Vrijdagavond ging FC Utrecht om 20.00 uur op bezoek bij hekkensluiter Heracles Almelo. Ook in de Keuken Kampioen Divisie werd er gevoetbald en Liverpool, Bayern München, Real Madrid, Napoli en Paris Saint-Germain speelden ook.

items.title: Ireland stay in Six Nations hunt with bonus-point win over improved Wales
items.summary: Ireland overcame a stubborn Wales to keep their Six Nations and Triple Crown hopes alive with a 27-17 bonus‑point victory in Dublin.

items.title: Liverpool avenge recent loss at Wolves with FA Cup win
items.summary: Liverpool bounced back from their recent defeat to Wolves with a 3-1 win at Molineux to progress to the quarter-finals of the FA Cup.

items.title: Mexico to deploy 100,000 security personnel for World Cup
items.summary: World Cup co-hosts Mexico plan to deploy nearly 100,000 security personnel to protect fans at this summer's tournament, amid ongoing cartel violence in the country.

items.title: Igor Tudor is flailing in the face of panic with Spurs staring at apocalypse now
items.summary: Relegation fears are sharpening while interim head coach grasps for solutions at a club hampered by inhibition, rage and injuries Igor Tudor’s messaging was always going to be key. All eyes and ears were on the interim Tottenham head coach on Thursday night and how he would react; the tone he would look to set. Would there be another blast for the players? Goodness knows, the material was there. It had been another impossibly awful occasion at the club’s home stadium, another defeat – this one by Crystal Palace. Spurs cannot buy a Premier League win at the Temple of Gloom; they have two all season, the basis for the worst home record in the division. As relegation fears sharpen to an incredibly uncomfortable point, the emotions in the stands ranged from apathy to anger. A lot of anger. Continue reading...

items.title: Who is to blame for Spurs' chaotic descent towards relegation?
items.summary: BBC Sport's chief football writer Phil McNulty examines who - or what - is to blame for Spurs' crisis as they fight to avoid relegation.

items.title: Bpost pakt uit ... met ex-speler Zulte Waregem, Antwerp, OH Leuven en Beerschot
items.summary: Kent u Charni Ekangamene nog? Hij was ooit profvoetballer bij onder meer Zulte Waregem, maar heeft nu een heel erg andere job. Hij is namelijk ... postbode. Een opvallende carrièreswitch, zoveel is duidelijk.

items.title: "Vriendelijk om uitleg gevraagd": Sven Vermant spreekt klare taal over zoon Romeo
items.summary: Sven Vermant speelde 414 wedstrijden voor Club Brugge. Hij volgt zijn Romeo van heel erg nabij op, al is zijn zoon totaal anders dan hemzelf.

items.title: Achtste (!) scoreloos gelijkspel helpt La Louvière niet veel verder tegen zeer matig Antwerp
items.summary: De 28e speeldag van de Jupiler Pro League is niet bepaald spetterend begonnen. La Louvière en Antwerp openden met een 0-0 gelijkspel, het achtste al voor La Louvière dit seizoen. Door de puntendeling zien beide ploegen hun beoogde doel steeds onmogelijker worden.

items.title: Is dat de juiste keuze wel, Bornauw die de touwtjes in handen heeft bij Anderlecht?
items.summary: Kenneth Bornauw heeft een dubbele en heel belangrijke taak bij RSC Anderlecht. De CEO moet op zoek naar een sportief directeur en een trainer voor paars-wit.

items.title: Dit zijn de uitslagen en doelpuntenmakers van vanavond in de Challenger Pro League
items.summary: Vanavond werden de eerste twee matchen van de 28ste speeldag in de Challenger Pro League afgewerkt. Dit zijn de uitslagen en doelpuntenmakers.
